poem: The flame flickers out alone in the dusk
Raindrops echo where laughter once lived
Silence hums louder than memory's cry
The sun blooms bright across the hill
Laughter dances through the open door
Balloons and wind in perfect play
Fists clenched tight in trembling rage
Words burn sharp as shattered glass
Doors slammed shut behind heavy breath
A sprout cracks stone, green and bold
Stars blink over weary shoulders
The path bends, but I walk still
The lake sleeps under moon’s hush
Soft winds cradle quiet leaves
Breath slows into morning calm
Nothing fits, not even the sky
Dreams spill backwards into thought
Questions grow louder than answers
The horizon forgets what it meant
Even joy tastes strange today
